steps.title: Spot the opposition
steps.content: The first sentence establishes the material's positive quality—flexibility for movement. The second sentence then introduces a negative quality—weight limiting mobility. These opposed effects signal the need for a contrast transition.

steps.title: Apply the signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast: the material's flexibility helps movement, but the embroidered feathers' weight limits it. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second sentence contradicts the positive quality just stated.
  • Choice A — "However,": signals contrast or complication of the previous point, so fits — the weight limiting mobility cuts against the flexibility benefit. ✓
  • Choice B — "Firstly,": signals the first item in a numbered list, so fails — no list is being introduced. ✗
  • Choice C — "In other words,": signals restatement or clarification, so fails — the second sentence introduces a new opposing idea, not a paraphrase. ✗
  • Choice D — "For this reason,": signals that the second sentence is a consequence of the first, so fails — limited mobility is not caused by flexibility; they are opposing qualities. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
